input:
My Lord, destroy them today.” When the Quraysh encamped, a group of them came up to the basin the Muslims had built. The Prophet told his companions not to oppose them. Every one of them who had a drink was killed in the battle, with the exception of Ḥakīm ibn Ḥizām, who was later to convert to Islam. Both sides sent spies to learn about the conditions in the other camp. ʿAmmār and ʿAbdullāh ibn Masūd, the Prophet’s emissaries, came back with this report: “There is an air of fear in their camp, so much so that if a horse wants to snort, they hit him in the face.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 17: Badr: The First Major Battle

output.scene_title: The Muslims Show Restraint at the Basin
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 17: Badr: The First Major Battle

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Late 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing simple white thobe with a brown cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by simple attire.

characters.name: ʿAmmār ibn Yasir
characters.appearance: Early 30s, light brown skin, slightly tall and lean build. Clean-shaven, dark eyes. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Slight weariness in his expression, eyes cast downward.

characters.name: ʿAbdullāh ibn Masūd
characters.appearance: Late 20s, medium brown skin, athletic build. Thin black beard, dark eyes. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Strong facial features, eyes cast downward.

output.moral_lesson: Patience and self-restraint in the face of adversity are key to success.
output.narrative_text: When the Quraysh came to the Muslims' water basin, the Prophet told his companions not to oppose them. He sent ʿAmmār and ʿAbdullāh ibn Masūd to spy on the Quraysh camp. They came back with a report that filled the Muslims with hope.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, backlit by sunlight. ʿAmmār and ʿAbdullāh ibn Masūd shown with full faces, expressions of weariness and determination. A sense of calm and restraint in the scene, suitable for children.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. A quiet 7th century Makkah oasis scene at dawn. A man in simple white thobe and brown cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, standing near the basin with sunlight casting a warm glow. Nearby, two companions in off-white thobes and brown cloaks, white head coverings, sitting on the ground with heads bent, eyes cast downward, expressions of weariness. Simple stone basin, date palm trees in the background. Medium shot, eye level. Warm golden palette. No modern objects.
